name: Software Saturdays
description: An 8-week certificate course teaching frameworks like React, sponsored by the College of Engineering.
longDescription: Software Saturdays is an 8-week, project-oriented certificate course designed to bridge the gap between academic theory and practical software engineering. Sponsored by the Purdue College of Engineering, the program teaches high-demand skills like Web Development with React, Git, and Cloud Deployment. Students from all backgrounds and experience levels are welcome, from complete beginners to those looking to master a new framework. By the end of the course, participants walk away with a functional personal project and a formal certificate recognized by the department.

sections.title: Project-Based Learning
sections.content: Every Saturday, we host workshops that combine lectures with hands-on coding sessions. We don't just talk about code; we build real applications. Mentors are available in every session to help debug and provide industry-standard best practices.

items.name: Foundations
items.description: Mastering the basics of the web: semantic HTML, modern CSS, and responsive design fundamentals.

items.name: JavaScript & React
items.description: Learning state management, component lifecycles, and how to build interactive user interfaces with React.

items.name: Deployment
items.description: Shipping code to the world. We cover Git workflows, CI/CD, and hosting platforms like Vercel and AWS.

items.question: Is it really only for beginners?
items.answer: While we are very beginner-friendly, we also provide advanced tracks for students who already have a foundation and want to learn more complex patterns.

items.question: Do I get a certificate?
items.answer: Yes! Students who attend at least 6 out of 8 sessions and complete their final project will receive a certificate from the Purdue College of Engineering.
